reviews.review_msg: Gordan is an easy going prof and he always comes to class cracking a joke. The lectures are a bit long (mostly reading the text out loud), and his lectures sometimes go off on a tangent. He is knowledgable about what he teaches. Grading overall is fair. There were two papers to write (1 6 pages, the second 10 pages min) and one midterm.
Gordan is an honest, great prof. He's kinda too easy on his students, but is incredibly smart.
Best professor! Knowledgeable, interesting and very funny. Attendance is very important to him. Exams are very straight forward so long as you do the readings.
By far my favorite class at SFU to date. Gordan is so funny, really passionate about the material and classes are very enjoyable! His exams are super easy as long as you do the readings.
easy marker; very helpful if u visit his office hours; if u only read the textbook u can get an A easily!

if u get the feeling he is randomly saying anything,is  unorganized...just hang in there...midterm n quiz r from text book n he clearly specifies whats required 4 presentation n paper in the frist 2 classes.very approachable in class/OH & quick with email replys.

do as what he says and u ll get a min of -A. he goes over what he expects from presentation in first class so dont miss that class. exams straight out of the txt bk. very easy n funny prof. though he seems a little unprepared/unorganized.
Very nice and helpful.  Classes are fun and follow the text VERY closely. Very intelligent and passionate about subject.  HIGHLY recommended.

very relaxed prof. very knowledgeble abt hinduism.the text bk is ur bible. for presentation make sure u do ur reasarch apart 4m wats in the text bk. do not expect good marks if u just say things tat r in the assigned text bk for the term. otherwise he is very easy.

If you're planning on taking this course for explorations, you might want to rethink it. the class is pretty boring, pretty, and textbook is really unclear especially for first year. The prof has a very thck accent and messy writing, but always tries to get the point across.
Really boring class (Indian religions), has a thick accent&bad writing = hard to understand the class in general. Nice/funny guy, but the class is terribly boring and he isn't the best prof
The class is fairly easy-going, and he is not a super hard marker. But his lectures are really unorganized and has a thick accent, and sometimes this makes it difficult for students to follow along.
